rationale: Entire career at IT-services firms (Accenture, Wipro) plus a fictional company (Dunder Mifflin), with only self-learner-level AI exposure and no production IR, embeddings, or ranking experience.

Career is entirely non-technical (Project Manager, Marketing Manager, Graphic Designer, HR Manager) with no engineering or ML work, and includes two fictional companies (Stark Industries, Dunder Mifflin), making this a clear honeypot.

Profile is a Content Writer and Customer Support rep with no engineering depth and roles at a fictional company (Wayne Enterprises), representing a non-technical marketing/writing background with zero IR or ML production experience.

8.7 years of backend/frontend engineering at product companies (Razorpay) shows solid software fundamentals, but the advanced OpenSearch skill is the only IR-adjacent signal while all ML experience remains at self-learner level with no production ranking or embeddings work.

Only 2.1 years of experience as a Full Stack Developer at Swiggy doing QA/test automation, with no production ML or IR work despite Kubeflow/MLflow skills that appear inflated relative to actual role description.

Self-described marketing manager background with Project Manager and Graphic Designer titles, no engineering experience, and a Ph.D in ML from a tier_4 institution that is contradicted entirely by a career history of customer support and mechanical design work.

8.6 years of frontend/mobile engineering with stints at product companies (Razorpay, Flipkart) shows solid engineering breadth, but no evidence of IR, search, ranking, or production ML work despite MLflow skills listed.

6.4 years of applied ML including production Elasticsearch and OpenSearch experience (advanced, 44-54 months), Hugging Face Transformers, and recommendation-system work at a startup (Saarthi.ai), though primary focus has been computer vision and the role was at large IT-services firm (Genpact) rather than a pure product company with owned ranking metrics.

Operations Manager and Marketing Manager throughout career with no engineering or ML work, and roles at multiple fictional companies (Stark Industries, Pied Piper, Initech) flagging this as an anti-persona/honeypot candidate despite AI keyword mentions in the summary.

Only 1.3 years of experience as a Frontend Engineer at Cognizant (IT-services) doing QA automation with no ML or IR work, well below the minimum experience threshold and entirely wrong profile for a Senior AI Engineer role.
